content: SQLAlchemy async session writeback from background task: Each asyncio task needs its own AsyncSession instance - never share sessions across async tasks. Pattern for background task writeback: (1) Import the module-level AsyncSessionLocal session factory (async_sessionmaker already created in database.py). (2) In the background task function, open a new session: 'async with AsyncSessionLocal() as session: result = await session.execute(...); row = result.scalar_one_or_none(); if row: row.github_team_slug = slug; await session.commit()'. This is the canonical pattern from SQLAlchemy async docs. expire_on_commit=False is already set on AsyncSessionLocal in this project - so ORM objects remain accessible after commit. Critical: the background task must import AsyncSessionLocal as a module reference (not at definition time) to support test reload patterns - already established in Phase 3.
